items.step: Snapshot the old durable plan and replace it with the linear-core refocus graph
Build the shared runtime bakeoff harness and semantic witness suite
items.notes: Use the same semantic suite and benchmark lanes for current fiber runtime and a linear alternative candidate.

items.step: Run the stackful-fiber versus linear-runtime bakeoff and freeze the backend choice
items.notes: This branch freezes the existing stackful fiber backend as the current implementation while the handle-based API, smoke checks, and three benchmark lanes define the comparison harness for any future linear alternative.

items.step: Introduce the prompt-handle public API shell and typed outcome and owner surfaces
items.notes: Public center becomes Prompt handles, reset, shift, Outcome, Pending, and Escaped.

items.step: Implement the ordinary linear pending-resolution core on the chosen backend
items.notes: No public driver, cancel, or discontinue surface in the new core.

items.step: Implement delayed escape for resume-later workflows on the same core
items.notes: Escaped owners remain the only advanced public feature beyond ordinary pending resolution.

items.step: Prove same-typed multi-prompt separation and alias rejection
items.notes: Prompt identity is per-instance handle identity, not type-tag identity.

items.step: Remove or archive driver, control-lab, and research-primary surfaces from the default product story and build
items.notes: Driver and control-lab paths are out of the default build and README; legacy source files remain in-tree but no longer shape the shipped product story.

items.step: Rewrite README, docs, and examples around the new library center
items.notes: Examples teach direct library use with prompt handles, multi-prompt separation, and delayed escape.

items.step: Rebuild closure gates and pass lint, tests, compile-fail, size, docs-sanity, and all three benchmark lanes
items.notes: Closure requires bench, bench-first-suspend, and bench-delayed-escape.
